name: The Gascon hillsides of Albret
description:
From Nérac, a former royal city on the banks of the Baïse River, this route takes you to the heart of the Albret region and its Gascon hillsides. As you leave the town, the road gently climbs towards open ridges, offering vast panoramas of the surrounding valleys. On clear days, the Pyrenees appear on the horizon.

The route crosses peaceful countryside punctuated by small villages. Francescas and Moncrabeau mark the route. The latter, known for its Académie des Menteurs (Liars' Academy), offers a lovely rural village atmosphere. From Moncrabeau, the route rejoins the Baïse Valley cycle route, which is more rolling, for a leisurely journey to Nérac.

The diversity of the landscapes and terrain makes this route enjoyable at any time of year. The starting and finishing points, Nérac, allow you to combine the outing with a visit to the historic center or a break by the water.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Nérac offers exploration of the Lot-et-Garonne department in southwestern France, characterized by gentle hills and fertile valleys. The region features the Baïse River, which carves picturesque valleys, and extensive vineyards. Cyclists can navigate quiet roads and dedicated cycleways, passing through historical towns and natural spaces like the Parc de la Garenne. This landscape provides varied terrain suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Nérac The most popular touring cycling route is Garonne and Canal Loop, a 16.7 miles (26.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a ride through tranquil rural landscapes and charming French villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Queen Margot Circuit, a difficult 59.9 miles (96.4 km) path. This challenging loop features significant elevation gains and explores historic Gascony with scenic river views. Local touring cyclists also love the Buzet Vineyards Circuit, a 48.3 miles (77.8 km) trail leading through flourishing vineyards, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Nérac is defined by gentle hills, fertile river valleys, and vineyards, with routes integrating historical architecture and quiet country roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides. The routes in Nérac are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1,700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nérac's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Nérac?
A: The Nérac region is characterized by gentle hills, fertile river valleys, and extensive vineyards. You'll find a mix of quiet country roads, dedicated cycleways, and some unpaved sections, offering varied terrain suitable for road, gravel, and trekking bikes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287162056/queen-margot-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen Margot Circuit&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, while others, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287126176/garonne-and-canal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne and Canal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer more tranquil, flatter sections.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Nérac?
A: Yes, Nérac offers several routes suitable for families. The region's gentle hills and dedicated cycleways provide options for easier rides. While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly detailed in terms of amenities, routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287126176/garonne-and-canal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne and Canal Loop&lt;/a&gt; (26.8 km, moderate difficulty), are generally more accessible for families.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Nérac is rich in history. You can cycle through Nérac's old quarter with its narrow streets and half-timbered houses, and visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4718016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henri IV Castle Museum&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henry IV's Mill&lt;/a&gt; near Barbaste, the Old Bridge (Pont-Vieux), and remnants of ramparts along the Baïse River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39986176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Caprais Cathedral – Baïse Aqueduct loop from Buzet-sur-Baïse&lt;/a&gt; also takes you past significant historical structures.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Nérac?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Nérac are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287162056/queen-margot-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen Margot Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287126176/garonne-and-canal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne and Canal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287228510/buzet-vineyards-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buzet Vineyards Circuit&lt;/a&gt;. There are over 40 identified cycling paths in the area, many of which are circular.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nérac?
A: The region benefits from a generally mild climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures for cycling through the valleys and vineyards. While summer can be warm, the riverside paths along the Baïse and Garonne rivers provide cooler options. Winter cycling is possible, but some services might be reduced.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through vineyards or offer wine-tasting opportunities?
A: Absolutely. The Baïse river flows through areas where vineyards flourish, producing local wines and spirits like Armagnac, Floc, Côtes de Gascogne, and Buzet wines. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287228510/buzet-vineyards-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buzet Vineyards Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you through these flourishing vineyards, offering a cultural and sensory dimension to your ride.

Q: What are the options for parking when accessing touring cycling routes in Nérac?
A: Nérac, being a hub for cycling, generally offers parking options within the town itself, especially near popular starting points or attractions. For routes that begin outside the town, such as those near Barbaste or Buzet-sur-Baïse, local parking is typically available in or near the villages. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for recommended parking areas.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes that connect to Nérac?
A: Yes, Nérac is strategically located to access significant cycling paths. The Baïse Valley by Bike (V82) stretches approximately 170 km and connects to the EuroVelo 3 (Scandibérique) and the Canal des 2 Mers, allowing for longer multi-day adventures. Some local routes also extend to nearby towns like Mirande or integrate into larger loops like the Great South-West Loop.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes and viewpoints can I expect to see?
A: The region offers picturesque landscapes of fertile valleys, gentle hills, and the winding Baïse River. You can enjoy riverside paths, woodlands, and clearings, particularly in areas like the historic Parc de la Garenne. Routes often provide panoramic views of the 'Val de Baïse' from hillsides, showcasing the town and surrounding countryside without overly strenuous climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2092387427/the-gascon-hillsides-of-albret" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gascon hillsides of Albret&lt;/a&gt; route offers particularly scenic vistas.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nérac?
A: There are over 180 touring cycling routes available around Nérac on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes 39 easy routes, 71 moderate routes, and 73 difficult routes, ensuring options for every touring cyclist.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Nérac?
A: The touring cycling routes in Nérac are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1,700 touring cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the region's quiet roads, diverse landscapes, and the charming historical towns along the way.

Q: Are there opportunities to cycle along canals or rivers?
A: Yes, the region is defined by its waterways. The Baïse River is a central feature, offering picturesque riverside paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287126176/garonne-and-canal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne and Canal Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes cyclists along tranquil rural landscapes and the Canal Latéral à la Garonne. The Baïse Valley by Bike (V82) also follows the canalized course of the Baïse River.
tips: Nestled on the banks of the Baïse River, Nérac offers a peaceful atmosphere. Formerly the residence of Henry of Navarre, the future Henry IV, the town preserves a rich heritage with its castle, cobbled streets, and half-timbered houses.

With its rolling hills, cultivated fields, and hilltop villages, this landscape of Lot-et-Garonne evokes the atmosphere of Tuscany in places. A peaceful and luminous setting, typical of the southwest.

Perched high in the Lot-et-Garonne region, Moncrabeau offers a superb panoramic view of the Baïse Valley. Known as the "world capital of liars," the village holds a lying competition every summer, organized by the Académie des Menteurs (Liars' Academy), a good-natured tradition that has made its reputation far beyond the region.
